blocks.content: The mage returned with a serious expression and conjured a sphere of fire between her palms.
Unlike Amy's multiple spheres, having just one made it easier to increase its density.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: He's focusing the firepower, isn't he? Desperate to win.
As Amy predicted, the mage was tense.
Of course, she had never quantified the power of her magic, so it was uncertain how high she could score.
But it seemed unlikely that she would struggle to triple the output of a Lightning Arrow.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Ta-ha!
The mage thrust her hands forward vigorously.
Under the control of the mage's Spirit Zone, inertia was ignored, so there was no need for special motions to cast magic.
Yet, adopting a shooting stance was the mage's flailing attempt to increase her focus even by a bit.
Amy followed the Fireball with her eyes. The fabric of the maze absorbed the fire, creating a whirlwind of flames.
The mage looked at the orb with hopeful eyes, but soon her face fell.
The score was 1,330. Just 310 points higher than the Lightning Arrow.
A significant difference for a swordsman judged solely on physical prowess, but in the realm of magic where phenomena were manipulated, it was much of a muchness.

blocks.speaker: Mage
blocks.content: How could this be? No! It's because the firepower was dispersed. It should have exceeded 2,000.
No one responded to the mage's voice.
The difference was too great. No matter what, she couldn't surpass Amy's score.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The archer thought it was already hopeless to pass through here. The girl who scored over 3,000 points must be the highest level here.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even a mage revered as sacred in the mercenary world scored only 1,330. That she exhibited more than double that magical power meant she was at least the most talented of her age group.

blocks.speaker: Rian and Tess
blocks.content: Not to belittle Shirone's skills, but Amy was his senior at school. If she, an advanced student, failed, it was expected that Shirone would struggle to surpass her.
Amy alone didn't think this was the end.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Shirone, you need to take this seriously. It might just be a narrow miss.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Got it. I'll give it my best.

blocks.speaker: Leader
blocks.content: Ha! What good is giving your best? It's about the results. It's over. Let's pack up and go.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone realized something from the leader's readiness to give up without trying. The fabric of the maze, known as the Eye of the Angel. What was her purpose in creating such an altar?
Shirone closed his eyes and performed the Sequence Method. A faint point of light emerged in front of him. Then, countless photons swarmed towards the center, increasing in size.

blocks.speaker: Mage
blocks.content: What, what's this? Photon Output? How is that supposed to make an impact?
The mage exclaimed in disbelief. Yet, she couldn't take her eyes off Shirone due to the unusual force not typically seen in regular Photon Output.
As Shirone opened his eyes wide, a fist-sized white sphere vibrated as if furious.
Gritting his teeth, Shirone fired the Photon Cannon. The beam of light stretched at near-light speed, striking the fabric of the maze as if to shatter it.
The unexpected number made everyone's eyes widen.

blocks.speaker: Mage and Archer
blocks.content: What in the world are those?
The mercenaries began to feel uneasy. They didn't know the principle behind Shirone's magic, but it was clear that none among them could withstand it.
However, there was no time to ponder further thoughts. Despite the tremendous impact, the fabric of the maze proudly emitted a red light, indicating failure.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone felt drained. What kind of orb was this?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Perhaps 5,000 points were the passing threshold. But it seemed unlikely that the Photon Cannon could be more powerful than this.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I wish I could try one more time.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Ho-ho! Do you understand how I feel? I felt the same. But really, how many points are needed to pass?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I don't know. Maybe a laser would work.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Oh, right! A laser can accumulate energy.
Shirone's laser vibrated molecules, increasing its destructive power over time.
Theoretically, it could destroy anything, making it perfect for this kind of assessment.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There's a next turn. We'll have to try after a full round. What about you? Are you not going to try?

blocks.speaker: Leader
blocks.content: Damn it! It's over! We have nothing left but to go back.
The leader walked towards the entrance with urgent strides, but no matter how much he twisted the doorknob, the iron door wouldn't budge.
The group turned around in confusion to see a man with white tattoos saying, 'You are one. If one receives an assessment, all must be assessed. You cannot leave before that.'
Understanding dawned on the group with the archer's translation. The rule wasn't just about unifying will but also about actions aligning.

blocks.speaker: Leader
blocks.content: Ah! This is so annoying! Why keep insisting on something so obviously futile?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Complaints poured from the leader's mouth, but to leave, he had no choice but to prove his worth.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So it is. This rule is truly cruel.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why hadn't he realized it until now? All the rules explained by the man with white tattoos were based on one premise.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Trust among comrades.
It was a peculiar evaluation method. In terms of firepower alone, mages had an advantage over Swordsmen.

But that didn't mean Swordsmen were weaker than mages. Even if a mage could shatter rocks and cleave the earth, a Swordsman specialized in biological combat could slice through that mage in one stroke.

Moreover, measuring the power of magic was problematic. If Amy, specializing in long-range magic, had attempted Sniper Mode, she might have scored much higher. But this place didn't consider such variables.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The narrow octagonal altar and the eight orbs.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why eight? Just for aesthetic balance? But in reality, didn't we all evaluate using just one orb?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What was most peculiar was the divine language. The fabric of the maze measured impact and displayed it numerically.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why? What was the meaning behind it?
If it was to distinguish those heading to Kergos, indicating pass or fail would suffice.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The score variance according to one's specialty could be vast. In such a situation, requiring everyone to be evaluated created a single emotion.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Obsession with scores. Anxiety when a comrade scores higher. Disdain when scoring lower.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Without a perfect understanding of each other's roles, discord is inevitable. And what awaits at the end of that rule is...

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The sacrifice of one to send another.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Shirone, let's just leave after this guy's done.

blocks.speaker: Rian and Tess
blocks.content: As expected, the leader was at a loss. He could sense a change in his fellow mercenaries' gaze.

blocks.speaker: Leader
blocks.content: Huff, huff, aaaaah!
Without pausing to breathe, the leader charged. Although he was sprinting, to Shirone, it seemed as if he was fleeing from something.
Was his Schema fully opened? Where was his sword positioned? How far was the target?
Without knowing any of these, his steps faltered, and he stumbled.
Gasping for breath, he swung his sword in a fall, and the imprecise blade struck beside the orb, bouncing off.

blocks.speaker: Leader
blocks.content: Damn!
The leader, attempting to escape the maze and the altar, hurriedly regained his balance and turned back.
The number 32 floated there.
But no one laughed. The comrades' expressions were as if they had faced a tremendous humiliation.

blocks.speaker: Leader
blocks.content: So what! It wouldn't have passed anyway! I did it, so let's just leave!
The leader exited the altar with a forced bravado, but his steps were directed away from his comrades.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That party is over. They'll probably disband once they leave here.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Could you translate for me, please?

blocks.speaker: Archer
blocks.content: Sure. What do you want to ask?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Has anyone ever passed through this gate before?
The man with white tattoos nodded firmly.
It's a gate meant for passing. It's not a trap.

blocks.speaker: Man with White Tattoos
blocks.content: The eight eyes of the angel will judge you.

blocks.speaker: Man with White Tattoos
blocks.content: The eight eyes of the angel will judge you.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What's the passing rate of those who've come here?
The man with white tattoos nodded firmly.
I don't know exactly. Sometimes many come, sometimes just one. From what I've seen, about one in ten pass. The rate seems higher when they're alone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone was startled. The rate was much higher than expected.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He had thought there might be no passers at all, assuming the maze's design by Miro wasn't just about assessing skills.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Of course, that view hadn't changed. But the man with white tattoos clearly stated there were passers, at a significant rate of 10 percent.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone's smiling lips quivered. A shiver ran up his spine.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone, why the sudden change?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Huh? What are you talking about all of a sudden?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Although Amy was puzzled by his words, Shirone was too absorbed to respond. Alpheas's voice seemed to echo in his head.
-Miro's world can be roughly understood. Not roughly, but clearly. If one couldn't overcome such a challenge, they weren't meant to learn about Miro.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone hadn't given up. He was determined to pass through here during the trip.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Amy, noticing Shirone's silence, guessed his thoughts.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Shirone hasn't given up.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Then she wouldn't either. Initially, she followed just to aid Shirone, but now she genuinely wanted to know about this woman named Miro.
